content: The phrase "In the beginning was the Word" refers to whom?
explanation: John 1:1: The Word is Jesus, who was with God and was God.

content: According to John 3:16, how did God show His love?
explanation: John 3:16: For God so loved the world that He gave His only Son.

content: What kind of water did Jesus speak of to the Samaritan woman?
explanation: John 4:10,14: Living water gives eternal life.

content: What does Jesus declare Himself to be in John 6:35?
explanation: John 6:35: I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger.

content: In John 8:12, Jesus declares Himself to be what?
explanation: John 8:12: I am the light of the world; whoever follows me will not walk in darkness.

content: Before Lazarus's tomb, what did Jesus declare Himself to be?
explanation: John 11:25: I am the resurrection and the life; whoever believes in me shall live.

content: What was Jesus's first miracle in the Gospel of John?
explanation: John 2:1-11: At the wedding in Cana, He turned water into wine.
options: Healing the man paralyzed for 38 years

content: At which pool was the man paralyzed for 38 years healed?
explanation: John 5:2-9: At the pool of Bethesda, Jesus said: Get up, take up your bed, and walk.

content: When many disciples turned away, what did Peter confess Jesus to be?
explanation: John 6:68-69: We have believed and have come to know that you are the Holy One of God.

content: On the great day of the feast, Jesus called whoever was thirsty to come to Him to do what?
explanation: John 7:37: If anyone thirsts, let him come to me and drink.

content: Jesus taught that true freedom comes from where?
explanation: John 8:31-32,36: The truth will set you free; if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.

content: How did Jesus heal the man born blind?
explanation: John 9:6-7: He spat on the ground, made mud, anointed his eyes, and told him to wash.
options: By laying on hands and praying
By placing mud on his eyes and telling him to wash in Siloam
By telling his parents to pray

content: What does the image of the "Good Shepherd" teach?
explanation: John 10:11: The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
options: He lays down his life for the sheep
He counts the sheep every night
He leads the sheep to pasture

content: What is the mark by which people will recognize Jesus's disciples?
explanation: John 13:34-35: By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.

content: The famous declaration in John 14:6 says He is what?
explanation: John 14:6: No one comes to the Father except through me.
options: The way, the truth, and the life

content: What does the image of the true vine teach the disciples?
explanation: John 15:5: I am the vine; you are the branches; whoever abides in me bears much fruit.
options: Abide in Him to bear much fruit

content: Why must Jesus go away so that the Helper may come?
explanation: John 16:7-13: When He comes, He will convict the world... and guide you into all the truth.
options: So the apostles may be free
So He may convict, teach, and guide into the truth

content: In the High Priestly Prayer, what did Jesus pray for those who believe?
explanation: John 17:21: That they may all be one, just as the Father is in the Son and the Son in the Father.

content: What was Jesus's final word on the cross according to John 19:30?
explanation: John 19:30: He said, "It is finished," and gave up His spirit.

content: What did Thomas say after seeing the nail marks on Jesus?
explanation: John 20:28: Thomas confessed Jesus as Lord and God.

content: In the opening of John, "In the beginning was the Word" (John 1:1), what is the Greek word for "Word"?
explanation: Logos (λόγος) means "Word, Reason," referring to Jesus as the Word of God.

content: In the famous "For God so loved the world" (John 3:16), what does the Greek word for "loved" mean?
explanation: Agape (ἀγάπη) means "unconditional love," the highest and purest love.

content: John 1:1 says, "In the beginning was the Word." Who is the Word?
explanation: John 1:1,14 - The Word is Jesus, who from eternity was with God and is Himself God.

content: John 1:14 "The Word became flesh" teaches which doctrine?
explanation: John 1:14 - The Word (Jesus) became flesh and dwelt among us — this is the doctrine of the Incarnation: He is fully God and fully man.

content: Why is John 3:16 called "the gospel in a nutshell"?
explanation: John 3:16 - For God so loved the world that He gave His only Son, that whoever believes in Him should not perish but have eternal life.
options: Because it is the longest verse
It summarizes the whole message of salvation: God's love, His Son, faith, eternal life
Because it is the first verse
Because it was written by Jesus

content: What did Jesus tell Nicodemus was the requirement to enter the kingdom of God?
explanation: John 3:3 - One must be born again of water and the Spirit. Not a second physical birth, but a spiritual one.

content: Jesus declared: "I am the ___ and the life"
explanation: John 11:25 - Jesus is the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in Him, though he die, yet shall he live.

content: Jesus said people will recognize His disciples by what mark?
explanation: John 13:35 - The mark of true discipleship is love for one another, not talent or knowledge.

content: Jesus said: "I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except ___"
explanation: John 14:6 - Jesus is the only way to God the Father; there is no other way.

content: What were Jesus's last words on the cross according to John?
explanation: John 19:30 - "Tetelestai" - It is finished! The work of redemption was paid in full.
options: Father, into your hands I commit my spirit
My God, why have you forsaken me

content: After Thomas touched the wounds, what did Jesus say about faith?
explanation: John 20:29 - Jesus blessed those who believe without seeing with their eyes — like us today.
options: Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed

content: What kind of water did Jesus speak of to the Samaritan woman?
explanation: John 4:14 - Living water = the Holy Spirit and eternal life. It satisfies the thirst for truth.
options: Living water — whoever drinks will never thirst again

content: Jesus declared: "I am the ___ of life"
explanation: John 6:35 - Whoever comes to me shall not hunger; whoever believes in me shall never thirst. Christ is the spiritual nourishment.

content: Jesus said: "I am the good shepherd; the good shepherd ___ for the sheep"
explanation: John 10:11 - The Good Shepherd lays down His life for the flock. A beautiful image of Jesus.

content: What does John 1:11 say about how 'his own people' responded to the Word?
explanation: John 1:11 — 'He came to his own, and his own people did not receive him.'
options: His own people did not receive him
His own welcomed him with joy
His own followed him to death

content: John 1:14 teaches the doctrine of incarnation: the Word became what and dwelt among us?
explanation: John 1:14 — 'And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we have seen his glory, glory as of the only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth.'

content: What did John the Baptist call Jesus when he saw Him coming?
explanation: John 1:29 — 'Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!'
options: The Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world

content: When Andrew brought his brother to Jesus, what new name did Jesus give Simon?
explanation: John 1:42 — 'Jesus said, You are Simon the son of John. You shall be called Cephas (which means Peter).'

content: On His first temple visit in Jerusalem, what did Jesus use to drive out the merchants?
explanation: John 2:15 — 'And making a whip of cords, he drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and oxen. And he poured out the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables.'

content: Jesus said: 'Destroy this temple, and in ___ I will raise it up.' Which temple did He mean?
explanation: John 2:19-21 — 'Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up... But he was speaking about the temple of his body.'
options: Three days — the temple of His body (resurrection)

content: Jesus told Nicodemus: 'Unless one is born of ___ and ___, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.'
explanation: John 3:5 — 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.'

content: John 3:8 likens those born of the Spirit to what?
explanation: John 3:8 — 'The wind blows where it wishes, and you hear its sound, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes. So it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.'

content: What famous statement did John the Baptist make about Jesus and himself?
explanation: John 3:30 — 'He must increase, but I must decrease.'
options: He must increase, but I must decrease

content: John 3:36 — what does it say happens to those who do not believe in the Son?
explanation: John 3:36 — 'Whoever does not obey the Son shall not see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.'
options: The wrath of God remains on him

content: Jesus told the Samaritan woman that God is ___, and worshipers must worship Him in ___.
explanation: John 4:24 — 'God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.'

content: John 4:35 — Jesus told the disciples to lift up their eyes and look at what?
explanation: John 4:35 — 'Look, I tell you, lift up your eyes, and see that the fields are white for harvest.'

content: At Cana, Jesus healed whose son with a word from afar?
explanation: John 4:46-50 — 'There was an official whose son was ill at Capernaum... Jesus said, Go; your son will live.'

content: At the pool of Bethesda, what did Jesus say to the man paralyzed for 38 years?
explanation: John 5:8 — 'Jesus said to him, Get up, take up your bed, and walk.'
options: Get up, take up your bed, and walk

content: John 5:24 — Whoever hears Jesus' word and believes the One who sent Him has what, and has passed from what?
explanation: John 5:24 — 'Whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life. He does not come into judgment, but has passed from death to life.'
options: Eternal life / passed from death to life, no judgment

content: Jesus said: 'I can do nothing on my own... I seek not my own will but the will of ___'
explanation: John 5:30 — 'I can do nothing on my own. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just, because I seek not my own will but the will of him who sent me.'
options: Of him who sent me (the Father)

content: John 5:39 — Jesus rebuked the religious leaders for searching the Scriptures but missing what?
explanation: John 5:39 — 'You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that bear witness about me.'
options: The Scriptures bear witness about Jesus

content: After the bread miracle, what did the people conclude about Jesus?
explanation: John 6:14 — 'When the people saw the sign that he had done, they said, This is indeed the Prophet who is to come into the world!'
options: The Prophet who is to come into the world

content: Jesus said: 'I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will ___'
explanation: John 6:51 — 'I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. And the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.'

content: John 6:53 — Jesus used a hard image about partaking of His life. What was it?
explanation: John 6:53 — 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.'
options: Eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man

content: When many disciples turned away, Jesus asked the Twelve, 'Do you want to go away as well?' How did Peter answer?
explanation: John 6:68 — 'Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.'
options: Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life

content: John 7:38 — 'Whoever believes in me, ___ will flow from within him.'
explanation: John 7:38 — 'Whoever believes in me, as the Scripture has said, Out of his heart will flow rivers of living water.'

content: When the officers returned empty-handed, what did they say about Jesus?
explanation: John 7:46 — 'The officers answered, No one ever spoke like this man!'
options: No one ever spoke like this man

content: When the woman caught in adultery was brought, what did Jesus say to the accusers?
explanation: John 8:7 — 'Let him who is without sin among you be the first to throw a stone at her.'
options: Let him who is without sin throw the first stone

content: John 8:32 — What will the truth do for you?
explanation: John 8:32 — 'And you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.'

content: John 8:36 — If the Son sets you free, what will you be?
explanation: John 8:36 — 'So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.'

content: John 8:44 — Jesus called the devil the father of whom, and a liar from the beginning?
explanation: John 8:44 — 'You are of your father the devil... He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks out of his own character, for he is a liar and the father of lies.'
options: Father of evildoers and the father of lies

content: John 8:58 — How did Jesus declare His pre-existence before Abraham?
explanation: John 8:58 — 'Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I am.' This was a claim to be the LORD (I AM).
options: Before Abraham was, I am (I AM)

content: What testimony did the man born blind give the Pharisees?
explanation: John 9:25 — 'He answered, Whether he is a sinner I do not know. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see.'
options: This man is surely the Messiah
I will follow Him to the end
Though I was blind, now I see
I do not know who He is

content: What did Jesus call Himself in John 10:9?
explanation: John 10:9 — 'I am the door. If anyone enters by me, he will be saved and will go in and out and find pasture.'

content: John 10:27-28 — What do His sheep do, and what does He promise them?
explanation: John 10:27-28 — 'My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me. I give them eternal life, and they will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.'
options: The sheep see Him — He gives blessing
Sheep hear His voice and follow — He gives eternal life; no one will snatch them
Sheep follow Him — He gives the promised land
Sheep eat with Him — He gives fame

content: John 10:30 — What does Jesus declare about His relationship with the Father?
explanation: John 10:30 — 'I and the Father are one.' The Jews immediately picked up stones to stone Him for blasphemy.
options: I was sent by the Father
I and the Father are one
tags: I and the Father Are One

content: How did Jesus describe Lazarus's death?
explanation: John 11:11 — 'Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I go to awaken him.'
options: Lazarus has fallen asleep; I go to awaken him

content: What is the shortest verse in the English Bible — also full of meaning?
explanation: John 11:35 — 'Jesus wept.' The shortest verse, expressing deep love.

content: At Lazarus's tomb, what did Jesus cry out with a loud voice?
explanation: John 11:43 — 'When he had said these things, he cried out with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out!'

content: At Bethany, who anointed Jesus with expensive perfume and wiped with her hair?
explanation: John 12:3 — 'Mary therefore took a pound of expensive ointment made from pure nard, and anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped his feet with her hair.'

content: When Jesus entered Jerusalem, what branches did the people take, crying 'Hosanna'?
explanation: John 12:13 — 'They took branches of palm trees and went out to meet him, crying out, Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!'

content: John 12:24 — What image did Jesus use about a grain of wheat?
explanation: John 12:24 — 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.'
options: Wheat falls and bears no fruit
A grain of wheat must die to bear much fruit

content: At the Last Supper, how did Jesus model service for His disciples?
explanation: John 13:5 — 'Then he poured water into a basin and began to wash the disciples' feet and to wipe them with the towel that was wrapped around him.'

content: John refers to which disciple as the one reclining at Jesus' side at the Last Supper?
explanation: John 13:23 — 'One of his disciples, whom Jesus loved, was reclining at table close to Jesus.' Traditionally identified as the Apostle John.

content: John 14:1 — What is the first comforting word Jesus said to His disciples after Judas left?
explanation: John 14:1 — 'Let not your hearts be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in me.'
options: Let not your hearts be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in Me

content: John 14:2 — In My Father's house there are what, and Jesus goes to do what?
explanation: John 14:2 — 'In my Father's house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you?'
options: One single place / nothing more needed
Many rooms; I go to prepare a place for you

content: Who is the Helper, and what will He do for believers, per John 14:26?
explanation: John 14:26 — 'But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things and bring to your remembrance all that I have said to you.'
options: The Holy Spirit — teach all things and remind of Jesus' words

content: John 14:27 — Jesus left the disciples what, not as the world gives?
explanation: John 14:27 — 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you.'
options: My peace — not as the world gives

content: John 15:13 — What is the greatest love?
explanation: John 15:13 — 'Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.'
options: Laying down one's life for one's friends

content: John 15:16 — Who chose whom first, according to Jesus?
explanation: John 15:16 — 'You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit.'
options: He chose and appointed the disciples
The Father chose them from eternity

content: John 16:24 — In whose name did Jesus teach the disciples to ask, that their joy might be full?
explanation: John 16:24 — 'Until now you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be full.'

content: John 16:33 — In the world you will have tribulation, but ___, for I have overcome the world.
explanation: John 16:33 — 'In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world.'

content: John 17:3 — How does Jesus define eternal life?
explanation: John 17:3 — 'And this is eternal life, that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.'
options: Knowing the Father — the only true God — and Jesus Christ

content: John 17:11 — What did Jesus pray the Father would keep the disciples in?
explanation: John 17:11 — 'Holy Father, keep them in your name, which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are one.'
options: In the Father's name — that they may be one as the Father and Son are one

content: At Gethsemane, whose right ear did Peter cut off?
explanation: John 18:10 — 'Then Simon Peter, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant and cut off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.'

content: Before Pilate, Jesus said His kingdom is not what?
explanation: John 18:36 — 'My kingdom is not of this world. If my kingdom were of this world, my servants would have been fighting.'

content: What famous philosophical question did Pilate ask Jesus?
explanation: John 18:38 — 'Pilate said to him, What is truth?'

content: Pilate pointed at Jesus before the crowd and said: '___'
explanation: John 19:5 — 'So Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said to them, Behold the man!'

content: Pilate placed an inscription on the cross in three languages saying what about Jesus?
explanation: John 19:19-20 — 'Pilate also wrote an inscription and put it on the cross. It read, Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews. ... in Aramaic, in Latin, and in Greek.'
options: JESUS OF NAZARETH, KING OF THE JEWS (INRI)

content: On the cross, to whom did Jesus entrust His mother's care?
explanation: John 19:26-27 — 'When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby... he said to the disciple, Behold, your mother! And from that hour the disciple took her to his own home.' The disciple is John.

content: After Jesus died, a soldier pierced His side with a spear; what flowed out?
explanation: John 19:34 — 'But one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and at once there came out blood and water.'

content: Which disciple entered the empty tomb and saw and believed?
explanation: John 20:8 — 'Then the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, also went in, and he saw and believed.' This is the disciple whom Jesus loved — John.

content: After His resurrection, Jesus said to the disciples: 'As the Father has sent Me, so I ___'
explanation: John 20:21 — 'Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, even so I am sending you.'

content: What did Thomas say when the disciples told him they had seen the risen Lord?
explanation: John 20:25 — 'Unless I see in his hands the mark of the nails, and place my finger into the mark of the nails, and place my hand into his side, I will never believe.'
options: I will go look for Him
Unless I see the nail marks, place my finger in them, and put my hand in His side, I will not believe

content: On the Sea of Tiberias, on which side did Jesus tell the disciples to cast their net to catch fish?
explanation: John 21:6 — 'He said to them, Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some. So they cast it, and now they were not able to haul it in, because of the quantity of fish.' They counted 153 fish.

content: What did Jesus ask Peter three times after His resurrection?
explanation: John 21:15-17 — 'Simon, son of John, do you love me more than these?... Feed my sheep.'
options: Do you believe I am the Christ?
Do you love Me? — Feed My sheep

content: What did Jesus prophesy about Peter's manner of death?
explanation: John 21:18-19 — 'When you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you and carry you where you do not want to go... He said this to show by what kind of death he was to glorify God.' Tradition: Peter was crucified upside down.
options: He would die peacefully in old age
He would live until the Lord returned
When old, another will dress him and carry him where he does not want to go (martyrdom)

content: In John 4:48, what did Jesus rebuke the official for?
explanation: John 4:48 — 'Unless you see signs and wonders you will not believe.'
options: Refusing to believe without signs and wonders

content: Where did Jesus tell the man born blind to wash to receive sight?
explanation: John 9:7 — 'Go, wash in the pool of Siloam (which means Sent). So he went and washed and came back seeing.'
options: The pool of Siloam (which means Sent)

content: When Philip asked to see the Father, how did Jesus answer?
explanation: John 14:9 — 'Have I been with you so long, and you still do not know me, Philip? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?'
options: The Father in heaven cannot be seen
Whoever has seen Me has seen the Father

content: In the High Priestly Prayer, what did Jesus pray believers might have so the world may believe?
explanation: John 17:21 — 'That they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be in us, so that the world may believe that you have sent me.'
options: Be one (as the Father and Son are one)

content: John's Gospel is structured around 'seven signs' of Jesus. Which of these miracles belong to those seven signs? (select all correct)
explanation: John's seven signs: (1) water to wine 2:1-11, (2) official's son 4:46-54, (3) lame man 5:1-9, (4) 5000 fed 6:1-14, (5) walking on water 6:16-21, (6) man born blind 9:1-7, (7) raising Lazarus 11:1-44. Withered fig tree appears only in the Synoptics, not in John.
options: Turning water into wine at the wedding at Cana (John 2:1-11)
Healing the royal official's son in Capernaum (4:46-54)
Feeding 5,000 with five loaves and two fish (6:1-14)
Cursing the fig tree that withered along the road (Matthew 21)

content: In John, the phrase 'lifted up' is used three times by Jesus to refer to his death. According to 12:32-34, what do the crowd's reactions NOT align with?
explanation: John 3:14 (Moses' bronze serpent), 8:28, 12:32-34 — three 'lifted up' sayings refer to the cross. In 12:34, the crowd protests: 'we have heard from the Law that the Christ REMAINS forever'. They cannot reconcile a dying Messiah.
options: They accept it as a prophecy of crucifixion on the cross
They thought the Christ MUST REMAIN forever according to the Law
They do not understand why the Son of Man must be lifted up so high
They link it to the bronze serpent Moses lifted up in the wilderness

content: John's Gospel uniquely records multiple Passovers. How many distinct Passovers are explicitly mentioned in John?
explanation: Three Passovers in John: (1) 2:13 — early ministry, temple cleansing; (2) 6:4 — feeding the 5,000; (3) 11:55-12:1 — his Passion. This is the main basis for the traditional ~3-year ministry estimate.
options: Only one, the Passover of his death
Two Passovers, at the start and end of ministry
Three distinct Passovers throughout his ministry
Four Passovers across three and a half years

content: John's Gospel opens with 'In him was life, and the life was the light of men' (1:4-5). Which subsequent claim of Jesus directly echoes this light theme?
explanation: John 1:4-5 introduces the Light theme. John 8:12 — 'I am the light of the world; whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life'. The two passages form a thematic inclusio.
options: In my Father's house are many rooms; I go to prepare a place for you
Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit
Whoever drinks the water I give will never thirst again forever
I am the light of the world; whoever follows me will not walk in darkness

content: In John, the 'disciple whom Jesus loved' appears unnamed multiple times. Which passages record his appearance? (select all correct)
explanation: John 13:23-25 (Last Supper), 19:26-27 (cross), 20:2-8 (empty tomb), 21:7,20 (Tiberias fishing) all reference the 'disciple whom Jesus loved'. Pentecost in Acts 2 is not in John and does not use this designation.
options: Reclining on Jesus' chest at the Last Supper, asking who would betray (13:23-25)
Standing at the foot of the cross with Jesus' mother, entrusted to care for her (19:26-27)
Running with Peter to the empty tomb and being the first to believe (20:2-8)
Seated at Pentecost preaching to three thousand listeners (Acts 2)

content: In John, Jesus repeatedly says 'my hour has NOT yet come' (2:4, 7:30, 8:20). Which verse marks the TURNING POINT to 'the hour has come'?
explanation: John 12:23 — 'The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.' This reverses the recurring 'hour has not yet come' (2:4, 7:30, 8:20). Jesus then speaks of the seed dying to bear much fruit (12:24).
options: 12:23 — 'The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified' (after the Greeks seek him)
11:43 — 'Lazarus, come out!' (at calling Lazarus from the tomb)
6:35 — 'I am the bread of life' (the Capernaum discourse)
4:26 — 'I who speak to you am he' (to the Samaritan woman at the well)

content: In John, Simon Peter progresses in his confessions of faith. What is his confession in 6:68-69 (after many disciples turn back)?
explanation: John 6:68-69 — 'Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life; and we have believed, and have come to know, that you are the Holy One of God.' The other quotes are Luke 5:8 and John 21:17.
options: 'Depart from me, Lord, for I am a sinful man!'
'Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life'
'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God'
'Lord, you know everything; you know that I love you'

content: In John 20:3-8, when Peter and the 'disciple whom Jesus loved' ran to the empty tomb, what detail about their order and reactions is recorded?
explanation: John 20:4-8 — 'the other disciple outran Peter and reached the tomb first' but stooped to look in without entering. Peter came after and went straight in. Then the other disciple entered, 'saw and believed'.
options: Peter ran faster, arrived first, entered first, and immediately believed Christ had risen
Both disciples reached the tomb at the same time and entered together
The other disciple ran faster and arrived first but did not enter; Peter came after and entered first
The other disciple stood outside weeping while Peter entered and was afraid to touch anything

content: John's Gospel contains seven distinctive 'I AM' sayings. Which of these belong among those seven? (select all correct)
explanation: The seven 'I AM' sayings in John: 6:35 bread, 8:12 light, 10:7 door, 10:11 good shepherd, 11:25 resurrection and life, 14:6 way truth life, 15:1 true vine. 'The rock' is not among these seven.
options: 'I am the resurrection and the life' (11:25)
'I am the rock of salvation' (not in John)

content: Nicodemus appears three times in John. In sequence, what is his FINAL appearance?
explanation: Three appearances of Nicodemus in John: (1) 3:1-21 at night, asking about new birth; (2) 7:50-52 defending him in the Sanhedrin; (3) 19:39-40 bringing 100 pounds of myrrh and aloes to anoint Jesus with Joseph of Arimathea.
options: Coming to Jesus at night and being taught about being born again
Defending Jesus in the Sanhedrin when they discuss arresting him
Standing in the crowd at Passover when Jesus cleansed the temple
Bringing about a hundred pounds of spices to anoint Jesus' body after death

content: According to John 9:6-7, by what specific method did Jesus heal the man born blind?
explanation: John 9:6-7 — 'He spat on the ground and made mud with the saliva. Then he anointed the man's eyes with the mud and said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam.' The man obeyed, washed, and came back seeing.
options: Spat on the ground, made mud, anointed his eyes, and told him to wash in the pool of Siloam
Laid his hand on his eyes and said 'Be opened', the blind man immediately saw everything
Spoke from afar 'Go home, your eyes are opened', without ever touching the man
Told the blind man to bathe seven times in the Jordan like Naaman the leper

content: John records two prominent women named Mary. According to John 12:1-8, what did Mary of Bethany do for Jesus?
explanation: John 12:3 — 'Mary therefore took a pound of expensive ointment made from pure nard, and anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped his feet with her hair'. This is Mary of Bethany (Lazarus' sister), NOT Mary Magdalene (who appears in 20:1-18).
options: Wept at the foot of the cross while he was crucified at Golgotha
Took a pound of pure nard, anointed his feet, and wiped them with her hair
Ran to the tomb while it was still dark and saw the stone rolled away
Stood by the Samaritan well and asked Jesus for living water

content: John records two miracles of Jesus on the Sabbath that caused controversy with the Jews. Which two are they?
explanation: John 5:1-18 — healed the man lame for 38 years at Bethesda on the Sabbath (controversial). John 9:1-16 — healed the man born blind, making mud on the Sabbath (controversial). Both led to debates about Jesus' authority over the Sabbath.
options: Healing the royal official's son at Capernaum and feeding the 5,000
Turning water into wine at Cana and raising Lazarus at Bethany
Healing the lame man of 38 years at Bethesda (John 5) and the man born blind (John 9)
Walking on the Sea of Galilee and healing Malchus' ear in the garden

content: According to John 18:33-19:11, what questions did Pilate ask Jesus during the trial? (select all correct)
explanation: John 18:33-19:11 records Pilate asking: 18:33 'Are you the king...?', 18:38 'What is truth?', 19:9 'Where are you from?', 19:10 'Will you not speak to me? Do you not know I have authority...?'. The Passover question is not in the text.
options: 'Are you the King of the Jews?' (18:33)
'What is truth?' (18:38) after hearing Jesus speak of truth
'Have you eaten the Passover meal yet?' (Pilate does not ask this)
'Where are you from?' (19:9) when hearing Jesus claim to be Son of God

content: According to John 13:21-30, by what specific sign did Jesus reveal who would betray him at the Last Supper?
explanation: John 13:26-27 — 'Jesus answered, It is he to whom I will give this morsel of bread when I have dipped it. So when he had dipped the morsel, he gave it to Judas... Then after he had taken the morsel, Satan entered into him.'
options: He dipped a piece of bread and gave it to Judas Iscariot; Satan entered Judas
He looked straight into Judas' eyes and called his name in front of the disciples
He wrote the name of the betrayer on the ground and all the disciples saw it
He told Peter to draw his sword and point at the betrayer but Peter did not listen

content: When Pilate asks 'So you are a king?' (John 18:36-37), what unique phrasing does Jesus use about the nature of his kingdom?
explanation: John 18:36-37 — 'My kingdom is NOT of this world. If my kingdom were of this world, my servants would have been fighting'. And 'For this purpose I was born, and for this purpose I have come into the world — to bear witness to the truth'.
options: 'My kingdom is on earth; I will defeat Rome and rule over this world'
'My kingdom is NOT of this world' and 'I have come to bear witness to truth'
'My kingdom will come in glory this very day if you believe in me'
'My kingdom is invisible and cannot be seen, and I am not a king'

content: In John 4:23-24, Jesus tells the Samaritan woman: 'God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in ___ and ___'. What two words and theological meaning?
explanation: John 4:24 — 'God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.' 'Spirit' refers to a heart renewed by the Holy Spirit; 'truth' refers to God's truth (especially revealed in Christ — 14:6 'I am the truth').
options: 'Sacrifice / obedience' — worship must include offerings and obedience to law
'Soul / strength' — worship must be wholehearted and never lazy
'Spirit / truth' — worship must come from a Spirit-renewed heart and conform to revealed truth
'Temple / book of law' — worship must have a sacred place and a guidebook

content: When Jesus declares 'I am the bread of life' (John 6:35), what specific promise accompanies it for those who come to him?
explanation: John 6:35 — 'I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger, and whoever believes in me shall never thirst'. The parallel 'hunger/thirst' structure refers to spiritual life, not physical immortality (cf. 11:25-26 on bodily death).
options: 'Whoever comes to me shall not hunger; whoever believes in me shall not thirst'
'Whoever comes to me shall have abundant material wealth in this present life'
'Whoever comes to me shall never die physically, but will live forever bodily'
'Whoever comes to me shall reign as king over Israel together with me'

content: In John 10:11-13, Jesus distinguishes the 'good shepherd' from the 'hired hand' by what core sign?
explanation: John 10:11-13 — 'I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep. He who is a hired hand... sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and flees'. The contrast: voluntary sacrifice vs. flight in danger.
options: The good shepherd wears white robes, the hired hand wears gray
The good shepherd owns more sheep, the hired hand has fewer sheep
The good shepherd is from the tribe of Levi, the hired hand from Judah
The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep; the hired hand flees when he sees a wolf

content: When Jesus tells Martha 'I am the resurrection and the life' (John 11:25-26), the accompanying promise has which two parallel parts?
explanation: John 11:25-26 — 'I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live, and everyone who lives and believes in me shall never die'. Two parts: (1) one who has died → will live, (2) one who lives → will never die spiritually/eternally.
options: 'The believer will perform greater miracles than me; will be wealthy in this life'
'Whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live; and everyone who lives and believes in me shall never die'
'Whoever believes will enter the Jerusalem temple; will see the Father appear'
'Whoever believes in me will never die physically and will never be sick'

content: Jesus declares 'A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another' (John 13:34). What makes this commandment 'NEW'?
explanation: John 13:34 — 'A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another; as I have loved you, that you also love one another'. Lev 19:18 already commanded 'love your neighbor as yourself'. What is NEW is the standard: 'as I have loved you' — Christ laying down his life for his friends (15:13).
options: It is the first command to love ever, never seen in any Old Testament passage before
The commandment requires loving enemies, not just neighbors as in earlier Law
The standard of love is raised: 'as I have loved you' (loving to the point of laying down life)
The commandment applies only to the New Testament era and is not binding on Old Testament believers

content: In John 14:6, Jesus declares 'I am the way, the truth, and the life'. What does the next clause in the same verse affirm as the core of salvation?
explanation: John 14:6 — 'Jesus said to him, I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me'. This is the New Testament's most direct statement of EXCLUSIVE salvation in Christ (cf. Acts 4:12; 1 Tim 2:5).
options: 'No one comes to the Father except through me' — salvation EXCLUSIVELY through Christ
'Anyone can come to the Father by various ways depending on their goodness'
'Only Israelites may come to the Father through me; Gentiles by other means'
'The Father will come to you on his own without going through me'

content: According to John 15:4-5, the metaphor of 'vine and branches' teaches what core truth about the Christian life?
explanation: John 15:4-5 — 'Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit by itself, unless it abides in the vine, neither can you, unless you abide in me... Apart from me you can do nothing'. The core: continuous union with Christ.
options: Every believer must work very hard to bear fruit worthy of Christ
The Father will prune fruit-bearing branches and give Israel rule over the world
Some believers are the true vine while others are a false vine not belonging to God
The believer must 'abide' in Christ, because 'apart from me you can do nothing'

content: According to John 1:1 (ESV), the opening verse has a three-clause structure. What is the correct order of those clauses?
explanation: John 1:1 (ESV) — 'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God'. The three clauses: (1) pre-existence, (2) relation to the Father, (3) full deity.
options: The Word was with God / the Word was God / In the beginning was the Word
In the beginning was the Word / the Word was with God / the Word was God
The Word was God / In the beginning was the Word / the Word was with God
In the beginning was the Word / the Word was God / the Word was with God

content: John 3:16 (ESV) has the structure 'For God ___ the world, that he ___ his only Son, that whoever ___ in him should not ___, but have eternal life'. What four words fill the blanks in order?
explanation: John 3:16 (ESV) — 'For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life'. The four key words: so loved / gave / believes / perish.
options: saw / sent / receives / be cast out
remembered / gave / honors / lose his land

content: In John 5:39-40 (ESV), Jesus says: 'You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life...' What does the next verse (5:40) rebuke them for?
explanation: John 5:39-40 — 'You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that bear witness about me. Yet you refuse to come to me that you may have life'. The issue is not the volume of study but refusing to come to the One Scripture points to.
options: 'You misunderstand the law of Moses and so misinterpret the Sabbath'
'You do not study Scripture diligently enough each day of the week'
'Yet you REFUSE to come to me that you may have life' (though Scripture testifies of me)
'You do not understand the original Hebrew of the ancient manuscripts'

content: According to John 10:10 (ESV), Jesus contrasts the thief with himself. How is that contrast precisely stated?
explanation: John 10:10 — 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life and have it abundantly'. A perfect parallel: three destructive actions vs. abundant life.
options: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I came that they may have life and have it abundantly
The thief comes by night while I come by day when the sun shines on the land of Israel
The thief will be judged severely by God while I will be exalted by the Father in heaven
The thief comes only to the Pharisees while I come to the Sadducees and to Gentiles

content: In John 14:2-3 (ESV), Jesus comforts his disciples before his death with what specific promise about his departure?
explanation: John 14:2-3 — 'In my Father's house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you? And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will take you to myself'.
options: He goes to test whether the disciples will truly love him in the future
He goes 'to prepare a place for you' and 'will come again and take you to myself'
He goes to leave this world and will never return to humanity again
He goes to Pharaoh in Egypt first, then returns to the people of Israel

content: According to John 15:13 (ESV), 'Greater love has no one than this, that someone ___ for his ___'. What two words fill the blanks?
explanation: John 15:13 — 'Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends'. Spoken in the Upper Room Discourse, this defines the love Christ shows on the cross — and he calls the disciples 'friends' (15:14-15).
options: lays down his life / enemies who oppose him
shares his life / spouse and married partner

content: John 20:30-31 (ESV) is the purpose statement of the entire book. According to John's own words, why did he write these signs?
explanation: John 20:31 — 'But these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name'. This is the mission statement of John's Gospel.
options: So that you may 'believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God' and 'have life in his name'
So that you may memorize the miracles and pass them down to future generations
So that you may know an accurate biography of Jesus' childhood in Nazareth
So that you may have legal evidence against the Roman authorities in court

content: According to John 2:1-11, what was Jesus' first sign at the wedding in Cana?
explanation: John 2:1-11 — at the wedding in Cana, Jesus turned water in six jars into good wine. This is the 'first of his signs' (2:11) — the first of seven signs in John.
options: Walking on the Sea of Galilee

content: According to John 6:9, how many loaves and how many fish did the boy have in the feeding of 5,000?
explanation: John 6:9 — 'There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are they for so many?' This miracle is one of John's seven signs.
options: Five barley loaves and two fish
Twelve loaves and four small fish
Three unleavened loaves and one large fish

content: The shortest verse in the New Testament is 'Jesus wept' (John 11:35). In what context did he weep?
explanation: John 11:33-35 — 'When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who had come with her also weeping, he was deeply moved... Jesus wept'. He came to Lazarus' tomb and showed deep love.
options: When seeing the Jerusalem temple about to be destroyed
When praying in Gethsemane on the night of his betrayal
When he came to Lazarus' tomb and saw Mary and Martha weeping
When he was crucified on the cross at Golgotha

content: According to John 1:35-36, who pointed to Jesus passing by and declared: 'Behold, the Lamb of God'?
explanation: John 1:36 — 'When he [John the Baptist] saw Jesus walking by, he said, Behold, the Lamb of God!' This continues from 1:29 — a title pointing to the Atoning One who replaces the Passover lamb.
options: The apostle Peter on the shore of Galilee when he first met Jesus
The prophet Isaiah in his vision at the Jerusalem temple
Mary Magdalene in the garden on resurrection morning
John the Baptist when Jesus walked by where he stood

content: According to John 4:6-7, when Jesus, weary, sat by the well in Samaria, what did he ask the Samaritan woman?
explanation: John 4:7 — 'A woman from Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me a drink'. The dialogue that follows leads to teaching about 'living water' (4:10-14) and worship in 'spirit and truth' (4:24).
options: Go and call your husband to come here
Go and tell the people of the city
Let me lodge at your house tonight

content: According to John 18:10, when the soldiers came to arrest Jesus at Gethsemane, what did Simon Peter do?
explanation: John 18:10 — 'Then Simon Peter, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant and cut off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus'. Jesus rebuked: 'Put your sword into its sheath' (18:11).
options: He fled the garden immediately with the other disciples
He denied Jesus right there in the garden before the soldiers
He drew his sword and cut off the right ear of Malchus, the high priest's servant
He wept and prayed for Jesus to be delivered

content: According to John 1:29 (ESV), what is the full statement John the Baptist made when pointing to Jesus?
explanation: John 1:29 — 'The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him, and said, Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!' The title joins the Passover Lamb (Exod 12) and the Suffering Servant (Isa 53).
options: 'Behold, the Messiah Israel has awaited from the patriarchs'
'Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!'
'Behold, the King of the Jews come to free you from Rome'
'Behold, the greatest prophet of all the prophets of God'

content: According to John 2:13-17, when Jesus cleansed the temple in Jerusalem (early Passover), what specifically did he do?
explanation: John 2:15-16 — 'Making a whip of cords, he drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and oxen. And he poured out the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables. And he told those who sold the pigeons, Take these things away; do not make my Father's house a house of trade'.
options: Burned down the merchants' tables in the temple court
Only stood and rebuked them with a stern verbal warning
Made a whip of cords, drove out sheep and oxen, scattered coins, overturned tables
Negotiated with merchants together with the disciples to relocate

content: According to John 4:46-54, how did Jesus heal the royal official's son at Capernaum?
explanation: John 4:50-53 — 'Jesus said, Go; your son will live. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and went on his way... The father knew that was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son will live'. This is the second sign (4:54).
options: By going to the official's house and laying hands on the child
By giving the child medicinal water from the pool of Siloam
By sending disciples with the official to pray over the child
By speaking from a DISTANCE: 'Go, your son lives' — the child healed at that very hour

content: According to John 5:1-9, how many years had the lame man at Bethesda been sick when Jesus healed him?
explanation: John 5:5 — 'One man was there who had been an invalid for thirty-eight years'. Jesus asked 'Do you want to be healed?' and said 'Get up, take up your bed, and walk'. The man was healed instantly.
options: Twelve years like the woman with the issue of blood
Forty years like Israel in the wilderness wandering
Eighteen years bent over by a binding spirit

content: According to John 6:51 (ESV), Jesus declared he is the 'living bread', and that bread is what?
explanation: John 6:51 — 'I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. And the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh'. This continues from 6:35 'I am the bread of life' and leads to the great dispute (6:52-66).
options: An unblemished lamb offered at the Jerusalem temple
My flesh — for the life of the world
Manna falling daily in the wilderness in Moses' day
The unleavened bread of Israel's traditional Passover

content: According to John 7:37-39, on the last day of the Feast of Tabernacles, Jesus stood and cried out that if anyone thirsts, let him come to him. What is the specific promise?
explanation: John 7:38-39 — 'Whoever believes in me, as the Scripture has said, Out of his heart will flow rivers of living water. Now this he said about the Spirit, whom those who believed in him were to receive'. This anticipates Pentecost.
options: 'They shall enter my Father's house in heaven'
'They shall see rain fill their cisterns'
'Out of their heart will flow RIVERS OF LIVING WATER' (referring to the Holy Spirit)
'They shall drink from the pool of Siloam all their days'

content: According to John 8:31-32, Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him: 'If you ___ in my word, you are truly my disciples. You will know the TRUTH, and the TRUTH will ___'.
explanation: John 8:31-32 — 'So Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him, If you abide in my word, you are truly my disciples, and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free'. The 'truth' = Christ himself (14:6).

content: According to John 8:36, Jesus continues the argument about true freedom. What follows?
explanation: John 8:36 — 'So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed'. This follows 8:34 ('everyone who commits sin is a slave to sin') and 8:35 ('the slave does not remain in the house forever; the son remains forever').
options: 'Whoever keeps the Law of Moses shall have true freedom'
'Whoever is baptized shall be free from sin'
'Whoever offers a sacrifice at the temple shall be free from judgment'
'If the SON sets you free, you will be free indeed'

content: According to John 9:35-38, after the healed blind man was cast out by the Pharisees, Jesus sought him out and asked what?
explanation: John 9:35-38 — 'Jesus heard that they had cast him out... he said, Do you believe in the Son of Man?... He said, Lord, I believe, and he worshiped him'. One of John's most significant worship scenes.
options: 'Do you BELIEVE IN THE SON OF MAN?' — and he said, 'Lord, I believe' and worshiped him
'Do you wish to be wealthy and powerful?'
'Do you wish to become my disciple and go preach with us?'
'Do you wish to take revenge on the Pharisees who cast you out?'

content: According to John 10:27-28, Jesus declared three things about 'my sheep' and himself. What three?
explanation: John 10:27-28 — 'My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me. I give them eternal life, and they will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand'. This is the 'assurance' of salvation that cannot be lost.
options: My sheep graze on mountains / they rest in folds / they drink at springs
My sheep HEAR my voice / I KNOW THEM and they follow me / I give them ETERNAL LIFE
My sheep multiply / the shepherd grows wealthy / the fold expands
My sheep are clean / their wool clothes me / their milk feeds me

content: According to John 11:43-44, when Jesus stood at Lazarus' tomb and finished praying, what did he cry out with a loud voice?
explanation: John 11:43-44 — 'When he had said these things, he cried out with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out! The man who had died came out, his hands and feet bound with linen strips, and his face wrapped with a cloth'. The seventh sign of John's Gospel.
options: 'Lazarus is my friend, return to me'

content: According to John 12:24, what metaphor does Jesus use to teach about death producing much fruit?
explanation: John 12:24 — 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit'. Jesus speaks of his own death bringing many children to God.
options: A grain of sand falling into the sea dissolves without fruit
A bird leaving the nest will find a better nest elsewhere
A lamp lit should not be hidden under a basket
A GRAIN OF WHEAT, unless it falls and dies, remains alone; if it dies, it bears much fruit

content: The opening of the 'Upper Room' section (John 13-17) is John 13:1 (ESV). What does it say about Jesus' love for his disciples?
explanation: John 13:1 — 'Now before the Feast of the Passover, when Jesus knew that his hour had come to depart out of this world to the Father, having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end'. This frames the love of the Upper Room and the cross.
options: 'Having LOVED his own who were in the world, he LOVED THEM TO THE END'
'He loved only the legitimate heirs of the Abrahamic covenant'
'He loved the Israelites more than the Gentile disciples'
'He publicly loved the apostle John more than the other disciples'

content: According to John 14:13-14, what does Jesus promise about praying 'in my name'?
explanation: John 14:13-14 — 'Whatever you ask in my name, this I will do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son. If you ask me anything in my name, I will do it'. The promise is paired with purpose: the Father's glory.
options: 'If you ask in my name, I will grant much wealth'
'Whatever you ask IN MY NAME, this I WILL DO, that the Father may be glorified in the Son'
'Asking in my name escapes all physical suffering'
'Asking in my name makes you a great prophet among the people'

content: According to John 14:26, what specific role does the 'Helper' (Holy Spirit) have when the Father sends him in Jesus' name?
explanation: John 14:26 — 'But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things and bring to your remembrance all that I have said to you'. Role: teach + remind.
options: He will lead disciples on missionary journeys to all nations
He will heal believers' physical ailments for life immediately
He will TEACH you all things AND BRING TO REMEMBRANCE all that I have said
He will give each person a new name in God's kingdom

content: According to John 14:27 (ESV), Jesus gives peace to his disciples with what specific DIFFERENCE from the world?
explanation: John 14:27 — 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid'. Christ's peace differs from the world's — not contingent on circumstances.
options: 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives DO I GIVE TO YOU'
'My peace is material wealth secured on earth'
'My peace is victory over enemies on the battlefield'
'My peace is given only to those with the greatest faith'

content: According to John 15:14-15, Jesus distinguishes 'servants' from 'friends'. What is the distinction?
explanation: John 15:14-15 — 'You are my friends if you do what I command you. No longer do I call you servants, for the servant does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all that I have heard from my Father I have made known to you'.
options: Servants obey while friends do not obey but only sit and feast
Servants do heavy work while friends only do light, easy tasks
Servants have no authority while friends have authority to preach
The servant does not know what his master does; I call you FRIENDS because I have MADE KNOWN to you all I heard from my Father

content: According to John 16:8-14, what works will the Holy Spirit do when he comes — both in the world and with the disciples? (select all correct)
explanation: John 16:8 (3 convictions), 16:13 (guidance into truth), 16:14 (glorifies Christ). Personal exorcism is not the specific role enumerated here.
options: Convict the world concerning sin, righteousness, and judgment
Guide the disciples into ALL TRUTH (not speaking on his own, but what he hears)
Glorify Christ — for he takes what is Christ's and declares it
Automatically exorcise the disciples' relatives' demons

content: According to John 16:33, what is Jesus' final word to his disciples in the Upper Room Discourse before the Passion?
explanation: John 16:33 — 'I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world'. Structure: tribulation is real; victory is also real.
options: 'In the world you must rely only on your own strength'
'In the world you will have TRIBULATION; but TAKE HEART, I have OVERCOME the world'
'In the world you will never face any difficulty whatsoever'
'In the world you must take up the sword and fight for me'

content: According to John 17:3, how does Jesus precisely define 'eternal life'?
explanation: John 17:3 — 'And this is eternal life, that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent'. 'Eternal life' = a KNOWING relationship with the true God, not merely endless duration.
options: 'It is entry into God's kingdom to live forever with the saints'
'It is material wealth and earthly peace throughout life'
'It is to KNOW the only true God and Jesus Christ whom you have sent'
'It is never to die physically but to live forever with one's family'

content: In the High Priestly Prayer (John 17:11), what does Jesus pray to the Father for his disciples?
explanation: John 17:11 — 'And I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them in your name, which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are one'.
options: Keep them from all earthly illnesses
Make them materially wealthy in this present life
Grant them a new temple in Jerusalem
KEEP them in your NAME which you have given me, that THEY MAY BE ONE, even as we are one

content: According to John 19:5, after Jesus had been scourged, crowned with thorns, and clothed in purple, Pilate brought him out and said what?
explanation: John 19:5 — 'So Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said to them, Behold the man!' The Latin 'Ecce Homo' is famous in Western art.
options: 'I find no fault in this man'
'He is truly the King of the Jews'

content: According to John 21:15-17, after the resurrection Jesus restored Peter through the question 'do you love me?' repeated three times. Which responses/charges ARE in the text? (select all correct)
explanation: John 21:15-17 — Jesus asks 'do you love me?' three times. (1) 'Feed my lambs', (2) 'Tend my sheep', (3) 'Feed my sheep'. The third time Peter 'was grieved' because asked thrice — paralleling his triple denial.
options: First time: 'Feed my LAMBS' to Peter the disciple
Second time: 'Tend my sheep' to Peter the disciple
Third time: 'Feed my sheep' — Peter grieved at threefold ask
Fourth time: 'Go and baptize the whole world entirely'
